planning_requirements: Select exactly one first Operations Visibility implementation or preflight slice.
Prefer the smallest repo-owned slice already identified as ready over a broader UI or browser-runtime reopening.
Preserve GitHub as canonical, `/home/olares/code/apex` as the authoritative host mirror, and `/home/olares/src/apex-power-ops-platform` as observe-only historical evidence.

Preserve Supabase change discipline: do not treat this planning packet as authority to mutate schema directly; prefer a later preflight/execution packet that can validate current database state, advisor expectations, and exposed-surface safeguards first.

Keep AI-services expansion, `ai_tasks` bridge work, hosting transition, auth widening, remote rewrite, rollback, force, reset, clean, and old-clone mutation closed unless a later packet explicitly requires and reauthorizes them.
Name one later execution packet and one bounded validation expectation for the selected slice.

decision_rationale: `PROJECT_STATUS.md` already marks the Operations schema tranche as ready to deploy and names `09_schema_additions.sql` plus `09b_enum_updates.sql` as the explicit next bounded surfaces.
Those schema files are repo-owned, finite, and directly tied to the Operations Visibility objective, making them a smaller first move than reopening broader UI work or browser runtime comparison.

A preflight slice is more truthful than immediate DDL because Supabase change discipline still requires current-state validation, advisor/security review, and a bounded execution plan before schema mutation.
This keeps the current Olares operating boundary intact while moving the active frontier back to business delivery work.
